PiperOrigin-RevId: 932771217

Use `common` instead of `build` for hermetic CUDA repo_env flags

Change `build:cuda_clang` to `common:cuda_clang` for the HERMETIC_CUDA_VERSION,
HERMETIC_CUDNN_VERSION, HERMETIC_NVSHMEM_VERSION, and HERMETIC_NCCL_VERSION
`--repo_env` flags. These variables are consumed by repository rules during
the loading/analysis phase, so they must use `common` to be applied across
all Bazel commands, not just `build`.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 931424028

Add HERMETIC_NCCL_VERSION="2.29.7" to the cuda_clang bazelrc config.

The rules_ml_toolchain NCCL repository rule requires this env var to
resolve the NCCL distribution for hermetic CUDA builds. Without it,
the GPU Docker build fails with "NCCL=None". The version 2.29.7
matches what TensorFlow and XLA use for CUDA 12.
